steps.title: Find the hypothesis signal
steps.content: The passage states the researchers' hypothesis directly: steel is more suitable than concrete for the lower floors of a tall building.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the hypothesis
steps.content:
Choice B shows the concrete‑floored building losing several inches of height while the steel‑floored one stayed taller, indicating steel better resists compression and settlement — exactly what more suitable predicts.

The other three either show steel deforming more, vibrating longer, or changing size in a way that does not demonstrate superiority.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
